scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

AT BENGALURU

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. 15,00,000/- WITH INTEREST AND COSTS UNDER SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 READ WITH ARTICLE 113 OF THE SCHEDULE TO THE LIMITATION ACT, 1963

PLAINTIFF: Ram Kumar, aged about 45 years, Residing at Jayanagar, Bengaluru. 
DEFENDANT: Suresh Patel, aged about 38 years, Residing at Koramangala, Bengaluru.


JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.
2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try the present suit as the Defendant resides within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court and the cause of action arose within such limits. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit claim falls within the pecuniary limits prescribed. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.


FACTS OF THE CASE

3. The Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a loan of Rs. 15,00,000 on 15.03.2024 via NEFT (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement between the parties, but the Defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages, the copies of which are annexed as Annexure B. 
4. In pursuance of the loan, the Plaintiff advanced Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant on 15.03.2024 via NEFT (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). 
5. The Defendant was obligated to repay the loan on demand; however, despite the Plaintiff's oral demands, the Defendant has defaulted in repayment and did not repay the principal or any interest. The date of default for interest calculation is 20.06.2024. 
6. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the loan within 15 days; a true copy of the legal notice is annexed as Annexure C. 
7. Despite service of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the principal or accrued interest.


LEGAL BASIS

8. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under Article 113 of the Limitation Act, 1963 for recovery of money lent, not secured by a mortgage, the loan being an unsecured hand loan repayable on demand, and the Plaintiff is entitled to recover the amount claimed herein together with interest. 
9. In the alternative, the Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under any other permissible statutory basis available in law for recovery of money due, and the Plaintiff seeks relief accordingly, to the extent permissible by law.


CAUSE OF ACTION

10. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the DefendantDefaulted in repayment, and the cause of action is continuing and subsisting for realization of the suit amount.


LIMITATION

11. The suit is within limitation under Article 113 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The three-year period from the date when the money becomes due (i.e., 20.06.2024) would apply, and the suit is filed within that period in relation to the default occurring on 20.06.2024, in accordance with the judicial guidance clarifying the commencement of the period under Article 113.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

12. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakh Only) for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee payable is Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- as per the Court Fee Context for City Civil Court, Bengaluru.


INTEREST

13. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 (date of default) till the date of filing of this suit;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at the rate of 12% per annum or at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable during the pendency of this suit under Section 34 of the CPC, 1908;
    (c) Future interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of decree till the date of actual realization.


PRAYER

14. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakh Only) together with pre-suit, pendente lite and future interest as claimed hereinabove;
    (b) Award costs of this suit including pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;
    (c) Award such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Sr. No. | Description | Annexure
15. | Bank transfer record NEFT (UTR: AXIB20240315123456) | Annexure A
16. | WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan | Annexure B
17. | Legal notice dated 01.07.2024 | Annexure C


VERIFICATION

I, Ram Kumar, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 14 of this plaint are true to my personal knowledge and belief, that no part of the cause of action has arisen in any other court, and that I have not concealed any material fa
